atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum payload builder crate declares the legacy ritual-async dependency needed by payload-building async transaction metadata and helper APIs.

destinations.snippet:
ritual-async-inspector.workspace = true
ritual-async-fees.workspace = true
ritual-async-registry.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The destination manifest declares several decomposed async crates but does not declare ritual-async.
Destination source still imports ritual_async, so the missing manifest dependency may be a build-surface gap rather than a purely obsolete dependency.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo check for reth-ethereum-payload-builder with the active feature set and assert ritual_async imports resolve from a declared direct dependency.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered by decomposition. lib.rs declares a local ritual_async module rather than importing an external ritual-async crate; active async helpers use decomposed async-inspector/fees/registry crates.

atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum payload builder crate declares revm-context so payload execution can construct async-context-aware EVM state.

destinations.evidence: The destination declares revm-context directly with async-context and fee-related features rather than using the workspace shorthand.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The dependency edge exists and is stronger than the source shorthand because it enables async-context and optional-fee features explicitly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build payload code that mutates AsyncContextFactory state and optional fee fields, proving the destination revm-context features are active.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered. revm-context is a direct dependency with async-context and optional fee features enabled.

atoms.source_snippet: alloy-sol-types, ritual-async-fees, ritual-async-registry, ritual-spc-signature, ritual-tee-registry-reader, ritual-precompile-codecs, ritual-async-inspector, ritual-sequencing-rights, ritual-scheduled-block-building, ritual-scheduled-verification
atoms.semantic_claim: The payload builder declares the core dependency suite for async fees, registry reads, SPC signatures, precompile codecs, async inspection, sequencing rights, and scheduled block building/verification.

destinations.snippet:
ritual-async-inspector.workspace = true
ritual-async-fees.workspace = true
ritual-async-registry.workspace = true
ritual-precompile-codecs.workspace = true
ritual-scheduled-block-building.workspace = true
ritual-scheduled-verification.workspace = true
ritual-sequencing-rights.workspace = true
ritual-spc-signature.workspace = true
ritual-tee-registry-reader.workspace = true
alloy-sol-types.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The destination manifest declares the same core dependency suite, plus ritual-precompile-addresses.

atoms.notes: This atom groups exact manifest edges whose behavior is covered by later payload source files.

atoms.source_snippet: ritual-metrics.workspace = true; ritual-heartbeat-tracker.workspace = true; ritual-scheduling.workspace = true
atoms.semantic_claim: The payload builder manifest declares metrics, heartbeat tracking, and the legacy scheduling crate for async/scheduled payload instrumentation and scheduling support.

destinations.evidence: The destination has the newer scheduled block-building/verification crates but does not declare ritual-metrics, ritual-heartbeat-tracker, or ritual-scheduling in this payload crate.

destinations.snippet: The source of truth is Scheduler parent state
destinations.evidence: Scheduling behavior appears intentionally decomposed, but metrics and heartbeat-tracker payload manifest coverage were not found.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run scheduled payload construction with heartbeat and metrics expectations enabled and verify the destination either emits equivalent telemetry/heartbeat behavior or documents the omission.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed missing support/observability surface. Scheduled behavior is decomposed, but ritual-metrics, heartbeat-tracker, and legacy ritual-scheduling manifest edges are absent from the payload crate.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload builder manifest declares once_cell and rayon for singleton/lazy state and parallel payload-building work.

destinations.evidence: The destination payload manifest does not declare once_cell or rayon.

destinations.snippet:
once_cell = { version = "1.19", default-features = false, features = ["critical-section"] }
rayon = "1.7"
destinations.evidence: The workspace dependencies exist, so the missing mapping is the payload crate-level dependency edge.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build payload modules that previously used once_cell or rayon-backed parallelism and assert there is no undeclared dependency or lost parallel execution path.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed obsolete support surface. once_cell and rayon are not declared because current payload sources do not use them.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload builder exposes an async-context feature that forwards to both reth-evm and reth-evm-ethereum async-context features.

destinations.snippet:
revm-context = { path = "../../../../ritual-revm-internal/crates/context", features = ["async-context", "optional_no_base_fee", "optional_fee_charge"] }
alloy-evm = { workspace = true, features = ["async-context"] }
destinations.evidence: The destination enables async-context at dependency edges rather than exposing the same crate feature forwarding.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Async context support appears always enabled through direct dependency features, but the public crate feature surface from the source manifest is absent.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build all consumers that enable reth-ethereum-payload-builder/async-context and assert the destination either supports an equivalent feature flag or no consumer depends on it.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered for behavior. async-context is always enabled through revm-context and alloy-evm dependency features, though the old public feature flag is not preserved.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload builder declares dev dependencies for ABI encoding and secp256k1 test fixtures used by payload and async transaction tests.

destinations.evidence: The destination payload manifest has no dev-dependencies section declaring alloy-dyn-abi or secp256k1.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run payload crate tests that encode dynamic ABI calls or sign async transactions and assert missing dev dependencies do not block the test suite.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed missing test-support surface. The payload manifest still has no alloy-dyn-abi or secp256k1 dev-dependencies.

adversarial_reviews.reason: Mechanical review completed; remaining non-exact atoms are now confirmed covered, confirmed missing, intentionally absent, or support/test/telemetry only.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Core payload async/scheduled dependencies and async-context support are covered through decomposed crates and direct feature-enabled dependencies; remaining omissions are manifest compatibility, telemetry/heartbeat, obsolete support deps, and test-support deps.
notes: Queue entry 87 was verified as M\tcrates/ethereum/payload/Cargo.toml; only diff lines 16809-16854 were analyzed for this record. Mechanical review review-files-0077-0089-mechanical completed; all prior needs-review atoms have definitive verdicts.
